summary: The number of families with children in DHS shelters more than doubled between FY22 and FY24, which the agency's own reporting ties directly to the wave of asylum-seeking families arriving in New York City since spring 2022. Growth leveled off in FY25 (18,652 to 18,767) as entries slowed and the city expanded exits to permanent housing, marking a stabilization after two years of rapid increases.
oneLiner: increasing overall, driven by the asylum-seeker surge; growth leveled off in FY25
